topic: Desert Shores Handyman: High-Ticket Repair Packages for Lakefront Wear, Gates, Trim, and Finish Work
excerpt: Desert Shores is a planned northwest Las Vegas community organized around a water-oriented recreation area and four man-made lakes, with a mix of single-family homes, townhomes, condos, and upscale lakefront properties. That changes the repair pattern a bit: in addition to the normal Vegas issues, we see more gate and hardware corrosion, trim wear around moisture-prone edges, and exterior details that need to look clean from multiple sightlines. Underneath, the valley’s native soils are still shallow, well-drained alluvial soils with carbonate-rich caliche, so tiny movement can show up as sticking doors, recurring wall cracks, and fence or gate drift even when the house looks solid overall. Then the climate does its usual number on materials — triple-digit heat, strong sun, gusty winds, and monsoon-season storm bursts — so the durable fix is usually a bigger package: tune the openings, reinforce the finish work, correct the water path, and reseal the exterior before the same symptom comes right back.
markdown_tables:
| High-ticket Desert Shores package | What it usually includes | Representative total |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Whole-house punch-list reset | doors, hardware, wall repair, trim, caulk, second-pass details | $2,500–$7,500 |
| Lakefront / moisture-edge exterior package | trim repairs, seal resets, hardware swaps, finish cleanup | $2,500–$9,500 |
| Gate and perimeter package | gate alignment, latch geometry, fence hardware, clean-close tuning | $2,000–$8,500 |
| Multi-room wall and ceiling finish scope | recurring cracks, wide floats, texture blending, prime-ready cleanup | $2,000–$8,500 |
| Pre-listing “show-ready” project | high-visibility fixes inside and out, plus final punch pass | $3,500–$12,500 |
| Project-sized handyman day scopes | fixed-price bundles rather than hourly one-offs | $1,200+ is common for larger projects nationally |
